_description: All hardcoded thresholds and weights for the scouting pipeline. Edit via the Editorial UI to keep a full rationale changelog.

ACTIVATION_THRESHOLD.description: Minimum composite score for a Technology→Vector ACTIVATES relationship to be written. Below this, the technology is considered insufficiently aligned with the transition.

COVERAGE_FACTOR_BASE.description: Number of aligned scalars at which coverage_factor reaches 1.0. Penalises activations supported by only one or two scalar overlaps. Raising this makes activation harder unless many scalars agree.

evidence_weight.description: Share of the composite signal_strength score driven by real-world evidence. The largest weight because observed transitions are the strongest signal.
evidence_weight.formula: signal = evidence_score * W_ev + scalar_coverage * W_cov + scalar_magnitude * W_mag + conviction * W_con

scalar_coverage_weight.description: Share of signal_strength driven by how many of the 26 structural scalars have been classified for this transition. Broad coverage implies a well-understood transition.
scalar_coverage_weight.formula: signal = evidence_score * W_ev + scalar_coverage * W_cov + scalar_magnitude * W_mag + conviction * W_con

scalar_magnitude_weight.description: Share of signal_strength driven by the average absolute impact score of the classified scalars. High magnitude means the transition causes strong structural shifts.
scalar_magnitude_weight.formula: signal = evidence_score * W_ev + scalar_coverage * W_cov + scalar_magnitude * W_mag + conviction * W_con

conviction_weight.description: Share of signal_strength driven by the highest hypothesis conviction score attached to this vector. Smallest weight — hypothesis confidence is downstream of evidence, not independent.
conviction_weight.formula: signal = evidence_score * W_ev + scalar_coverage * W_cov + scalar_magnitude * W_mag + conviction * W_con

evidence_tanh_divisor.description: Divisor D in tanh(evidence_count / D). Controls how quickly evidence saturates. At D=3: 1 piece → 0.31, 3 pieces → 0.76, 6 pieces → 0.96. Raise D to require more evidence before saturation.

scalar_coverage_target.description: Number of classified scalars at which scalar_coverage reaches 1.0. Set to 8 because we consider a transition 'well-characterised' once a third of the 26 scalars are classified.

SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD.description: Semantic similarity above which a new Business Model candidate is blocked as a duplicate. The enrichment LLM returns a similarity_score; if it meets this threshold the node is not created.

REVIEW_THRESHOLD.description: Semantic similarity above which a new Business Model is allowed but flagged pending_human_review=true. Creates a HumanReviewItem in the queue so an analyst can decide.
REVIEW_THRESHOLD.formula: if similarity_score >= REVIEW_THRESHOLD: flag for review

MIN_VECTORS_DEFAULT.description: Minimum number of distinct TransformationVectors a scalar must appear in to qualify as a macro trend. Set to 2 so isolated activations do not create false trend signals.

MIN_SIGNAL.description: Minimum signal_strength a vector must have to be included in trend analysis. Currently 0.0 so all vectors are included regardless of data completeness. Raise once the graph is well-populated.

IMPACT_SCORE.description: Integer score assigned to each (direction, strength) combination when a scalar is impacted by a transition or technology. Used in vector activation scoring and trend magnitude calculations. JSON keys use underscore-joined format; code converts to tuple keys at import time.
